info.name: EasyRisparmio API
info.description:
API collection for the EasyRisparmio Italian energy comparison & switching platform.

## Modules
- **Authentication** — Email/password + social login (Google, Facebook, Apple via Firebase)
- **Users** — User profile management and admin CRUD
- **Referrals** — Invite & Earn referral program for users and admin management
- **Meters** — Utility meter management (electricity, gas, water, internet) with admin moderation
- **Suppliers** — Energy supplier management with public listing and admin CRUD
- **Offers** — Energy offer management, comparison, recommendations, and admin moderation
- **Agreements** — Exclusive partner discounts & agreements for mobile app users
- **Support** — Support tickets, messaging, and FAQ management
- **Bills** — Energy bill upload, analysis, and admin management
- **Cases** — Supplier switching requests, documents, and case management
- **Contracts** — Contract management for switching cases (admin)
- **Commissions** — Commission tracking, rules, and payouts (admin)
- **Notifications** — Push notifications, in-app notifications, and admin broadcast
- **Dashboard** — Admin KPIs and user dashboard summary
- **Market Data** — Italian energy market indices (PUN, PSV, GME)
- **File Upload** — General-purpose file upload endpoint

## Roles
| Role | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `personal` | Individual user (default) |
| `business` | Business user with company profile |
| `admin` | Platform administrator (seeded, cannot register) |

## Token Flow
1. Login or social-login returns `accessToken` (JWT, 15 min) + `refreshToken` (UUID, 7 days)
2. Use `accessToken` in `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header for protected endpoints
3. When expired, call refresh-token to get a new pair

## Setup
1. Set `baseUrl` variable to your server (default: `http://localhost:3000`)
2. Register a user, then login — tokens are auto-saved to collection variables
3. All protected requests automatically use the saved `accessToken`

item.name: Authentication
item.description: Email/password + social login (Google, Facebook, Apple via Firebase). JWT access tokens (15 min) + UUID refresh tokens (7 days). OTP verification and password reset flows.

item.name: Registration
item.description: Single registration endpoint for both personal and business users. The `role` field determines which fields are required. After registration, the account status is `pending_verification` until the email OTP is verified. Admin role cannot be used. An optional `referralCode` can be provided to link the new user to a referrer.

request.description:
Creates a new user account (personal or business).

The `role` field determines which fields are required. When `role` is `business`, the business-specific fields (`companyName`, `partitaIva`, etc.) become required via conditional validation (`@ValidateIf`).

After registration, a 6-digit OTP is generated for email verification. The account status is `pending_verification` until the OTP is verified via `POST /auth/verify-otp`.

**Body Fields (always required):**
- `email` — (required) valid email address, must be unique across all users
- `password` — (required) min 8 chars, must include uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character
- `firstName` — (required) max 100 characters
- `lastName` — (required) max 100 characters
- `role` — (required) Enum: `personal`, `business`. Determines which additional fields are required. Note: `admin` is not allowed

**Body Fields (optional for all roles):**
- `phone` — (optional) phone number, max 20 characters
- `referralCode` — (optional) 8-character code from an existing user. If valid, the referral is linked automatically

**Body Fields (required when role is `business`):**
- `companyName` — (required if business) company legal name, max 255 characters
- `partitaIva` — (required if business) Italian VAT number, must be exactly 11 digits (e.g. `12345678901`)

**Body Fields (optional when role is `business`):**
- `pecEmail` — (optional) PEC certified email address (Posta Elettronica Certificata), used for official communications in Italy
- `legalRepresentative` — (optional) full name of the company's legal representative, max 255 characters
- `companyType` — (optional) business entity type, e.g. `S.r.l.`, `S.p.A.`, `S.n.c.`, `S.a.s.`, max 100 characters
- `atecoCode` — (optional) ATECO economic activity code (Italian NACE equivalent), e.g. `35.11.00` for electricity production, max 10 characters

item.name: Login
item.description: Authentication endpoints for email/password and social login. Both return a JWT access token and UUID refresh token on success.

request.description:
Authenticates with email and password. Returns JWT access token (15 min) and UUID refresh token (7 days).

**Login is blocked when:**
- Invalid credentials
- Account `pending_verification` or `suspended`
- Social-only user (no password set)

**Auto-saves** `accessToken` and `refreshToken` to collection variables.

response.name: 401 - Email Not Verified

request.description:
Login or register via Google, Facebook, or Apple using Firebase Authentication.

**Body Fields:**
- `idToken` — (required) Firebase ID token obtained from the mobile app after authenticating with a social provider (Google, Facebook, or Apple). Must be a valid, unexpired token. The server verifies it with Firebase Admin SDK.

**Behavior:**
- **New user** — account created with `role: personal`, `status: active`, `emailVerified: true`
- **Existing email** — Firebase UID linked to existing account (account linking)
- **No email on social account** — returns 400 error

**Auto-saves** `accessToken` and `refreshToken` to collection variables.

response.name: 400 - No Email on Social Account

item.name: OTP & Password
item.description: OTP verification and password reset flows.

request.description:
Verifies a 6-digit OTP code sent to the user's email.

**Body Fields:**
- `email` — (required) the email address the OTP was sent to
- `code` — (required) 6-digit numeric OTP code (sent as string, e.g. `"123456"`)
- `type` — (required) Enum: `email_verification`, `phone_verification`, `password_reset`. Determines which verification flow to complete.
  - `email_verification` — activates the account (status changes from `pending_verification` to `active`)
  - `phone_verification` — marks phone as verified
  - `password_reset` — validates OTP for password reset (does NOT activate the account)

**Behavior:** 10-minute expiry, single-use, previous unused OTPs of same type are invalidated. Maximum 5 failed attempts before lockout.
Requests a password reset OTP. Always returns the same response regardless of whether the email exists (prevents user enumeration).

response.name: 200 - Reset OTP Sent

request.description:
Resets password using OTP from forgot-password.

**Body Fields:**
- `email` — (required) the email address the OTP was sent to
- `code` — (required) 6-digit numeric OTP code from the forgot-password request
- `newPassword` — (required) min 8 chars, must include uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character (same rules as registration)

After success, all existing refresh tokens are revoked (forces re-login on all devices).

Exchanges a valid refresh token for a new token pair. Old token is revoked (rotation).

**Body Fields:**
- `refreshToken` — (required) UUID refresh token (7-day lifetime), NOT the JWT access token. Obtained from login or previous refresh.

**Auto-saves** new `accessToken` and `refreshToken` to collection variables.
Returns the authenticated user's profile. Requires JWT access token. `passwordHash` is never included.

item.name: Referrals
item.description: Invite & Earn referral program. Users share referral codes to invite friends and earn EUR 10 per qualified referral. Admin manages the referral lifecycle: PENDING -> REGISTERED -> QUALIFIED -> REWARDED.

item.name: User
item.description:
User-facing referral endpoints for the Invite & Earn program. Available to `personal` and `business` users.

**Flow:**
1. Get your referral code via `GET /referrals/my-code`
2. Share the link or send targeted invites via `POST /referrals/invite`
3. Track your referrals via `GET /referrals/my-referrals`
4. Earn €10 per qualified referral when admin approves

request.description:
Returns the user's unique 8-character referral code, a shareable registration link, and aggregate referral stats.

If the user doesn't have a code yet, one is auto-generated on the first call.

**Stats include:**
- `totalInvites` — total referrals created
- `registered` — referred users who signed up
- `qualified` — referrals that met qualification criteria
- `rewarded` — referrals where reward was credited
- `totalEarnings` — total EUR earned from referrals

query.description: Filter by referral status. Enum: pending, registered, qualified, rewarded, expired (optional)

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of referrals created by the authenticated user. Each referral shows status, referred email/phone, and referred user details if they registered.

**Query Parameters:**
- `page` — page number (default: 1)
- `limit` — items per page (default: 20, max: 100)
- `status` — optional filter: `pending`, `registered`, `qualified`, `rewarded`, `expired`

request.description:
Creates a new referral invite with `pending` status.

**Body Fields:**
- `referredEmail` — (optional) email address of the person being invited. Must be a valid email if provided
- `referredPhone` — (optional) phone number of the person being invited, max 20 characters

**Notes:**
- **With email/phone** — targeted invite for a specific person
- **Without both** — generic invite (anyone with the code can use it)
- Invites expire after **90 days**
- The user's referral code is auto-generated if they don't have one yet

**Auto-saves** `referralId` to collection variables for use in admin endpoints.

item.name: Admin
item.description:
Admin endpoints for managing the referral program. Requires `admin` role.

**Status lifecycle:** PENDING → REGISTERED → QUALIFIED → REWARDED (any → EXPIRED)

**KPIs** include total counts by status and total rewards paid in EUR.

request.description:
Returns aggregate KPIs for the entire referral program: total referrals, breakdown by status, and total rewards paid in EUR.

Requires **admin** role.

query.description: Filter by referral status. Enum: pending, registered, qualified, rewarded, expired (optional)
Filter from date, ISO 8601 format e.g. 2026-01-01 (optional)
Filter to date, ISO 8601 format e.g. 2026-12-31 (optional)
Search by referrer name, email, or referral code (optional)

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of all referrals with referrer and referred user details.

**Filters (all optional):**
- `status` — `pending`, `registered`, `qualified`, `rewarded`, `expired`
- `referrerId` — UUID of the referrer user
- `dateFrom` / `dateTo` — ISO 8601 date range on created_at
- `search` — matches referrer name, email, or referral code

Requires **admin** role.

Returns a single referral with full referrer and referred user details.

Requires **admin** role. The `referralId` variable is auto-set by the Create Invite request.

Updates a referral's status to `qualified`.

**Body Fields:**
- `status` — (required) Enum: `pending`, `registered`, `qualified`, `rewarded`, `expired`. The target status for this referral.

**Valid transitions:**
- `pending` → `registered` or `expired`
- `registered` → `qualified` or `expired`
- `qualified` → `rewarded` or `expired`
- `rewarded` and `expired` are terminal states

Requires **admin** role.

response.name: 400 - Invalid Status Transition

request.description:
Updates a referral's status to `rewarded` and credits the reward amount.

**Body Fields:**
- `status` — (required) Enum: `pending`, `registered`, `qualified`, `rewarded`, `expired`. Must be `rewarded` for this endpoint variant.
- `rewardAmount` — (required when status is `rewarded`) reward amount in EUR (decimal). The `rewardCreditedAt` timestamp is set automatically.

Only valid from `qualified` status. Requires **admin** role.

response.name: 400 - Missing rewardAmount
400 - Invalid Transition (not from qualified)

request.description:
Updates a referral's status to `expired`.

**Body Fields:**
- `status` — (required) Enum: `pending`, `registered`, `qualified`, `rewarded`, `expired`. Must be `expired` for this endpoint variant.

Can be applied from any non-terminal status (`pending`, `registered`, `qualified`). Requires **admin** role.

response.name: 400 - Cannot Expire Terminal State

item.name: Meters

item.name: User

User-facing meter/utility endpoints. Returns only `active` meters for the authenticated user.

Users typically have 2-5 utility meters (electricity, gas, water, internet). No pagination needed.

request.description:
Returns the authenticated user's active meters with supplier and address details.

Only meters with `active` status are returned. Meters in `pending`, `inactive`, or `terminated` status are hidden from the user.

No pagination — users typically have 2-5 utilities.

item.name: Admin
item.description:
Admin endpoints for managing utility meters. Requires `admin` role.

**Utility types:** `electricity` (POD), `gas` (PDR), `water`, `internet`

**Status lifecycle (moderation):**
- `pending` → `active` or `terminated`
- `active` → `inactive` or `terminated`
- `inactive` → `active` or `terminated`
- `terminated` = permanent (no further transitions)

Only `active` meters are visible to users. Admin uses status to moderate visibility.

request.description:
Creates a new utility meter and assigns it to a user.

**Body Fields:**
- `userId` — (required) UUID of the user who owns this meter
- `utilityType` — (required) Enum: `electricity`, `gas`, `water`, `internet`. Type of utility service
- `meterCode` — (required) unique meter identifier (POD code for electricity, PDR code for gas), max 50 characters
- `supplierId` — (optional) UUID of the energy supplier for this meter
- `status` — (optional) Enum: `active`, `inactive`, `pending`, `terminated`. Defaults to `active`
- `annualConsumption` — (optional) estimated annual consumption, decimal (precision 10, scale 2)
- `consumptionUnit` — (optional) unit of measurement, e.g. `kWh` for electricity, `Smc` for gas, max 20 characters
- `contractedPowerKw` — (optional) contracted power in kW (relevant for electricity meters only), decimal
- `addressId` — (optional) UUID of the address where the meter is located
- `activationDate` — (optional) meter activation date in ISO 8601 format, e.g. `2025-01-15`
- `notes` — (optional) free-text notes

Requires **admin** role.

response.name: 400 - Validation Error
409 - Duplicate Meter Code

query.description: Filter by utility type. Enum: electricity, gas, water, internet (optional)
Filter by meter status. Enum: active, inactive, pending, terminated (optional)
Search by meter code, POD/PDR number, or user name (optional)

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of all meters with user, supplier, and address relations.

**Filters (all optional):**
- `utilityType` — `electricity`, `gas`, `water`, `internet`
- `status` — `active`, `inactive`, `pending`, `terminated`
- `userId` — filter by meter owner
- `supplierId` — filter by supplier
- `search` — matches meter code, user name, or email

Requires **admin** role.

Returns a single meter with full user, supplier, and address details.

Requires **admin** role. The `meterId` variable is auto-set by the Create Meter request.

request.description:
Updates an existing meter's details. All fields are optional — only provided fields are updated.

**Body Fields (all optional):**
- `userId` — UUID of the user who owns this meter
- `utilityType` — Enum: `electricity`, `gas`, `water`, `internet`
- `meterCode` — unique meter identifier, max 50 characters
- `supplierId` — UUID of the energy supplier
- `status` — Enum: `active`, `inactive`, `pending`, `terminated`
- `annualConsumption` — estimated annual consumption, decimal
- `consumptionUnit` — unit of measurement (e.g. `kWh`, `Smc`), max 20 characters
- `contractedPowerKw` — contracted power in kW, decimal
- `addressId` — UUID of the address where the meter is located
- `activationDate` — ISO 8601 date
- `notes` — free-text notes

Requires **admin** role.

item.name: Admin
item.description:
Admin endpoints for managing energy offers. Requires `admin` role.

**Offer status lifecycle (moderation):**
- `draft` → `active` or `archived`
- `active` → `expiring` or `archived`
- `expiring` → `expired` or `archived`
- `expired` → `archived`
- `archived` = permanent (no further transitions)

Only `active` and `expiring` offers are visible to public users.

request.description:
Creates a new energy offer.

**Body Fields:**
- `name` — (required) offer name, max 255 characters
- `description` — (optional) detailed description of the offer
- `energyType` — (required) Enum: `electricity`, `gas`, `dual`. Type of energy this offer covers
- `marketType` — (required) Enum: `fixed`, `variable`, `indexed`. Pricing model type
- `pricePerKwh` — (optional) price per kilowatt-hour in EUR, decimal (precision 10, scale 6). Used for electricity offers
- `pricePerSmc` — (optional) price per standard cubic meter in EUR, decimal. Used for gas offers
- `fixedMonthlyFee` — (required) fixed monthly fee in EUR, decimal (precision 10, scale 2, default: 0)
- `activationCost` — (required) one-time activation cost in EUR, decimal (precision 10, scale 2, default: 0)
- `contractDurationMonths` — (required) contract duration in months, integer (min: 1)
- `isGreenEnergy` — (optional) boolean, whether the offer uses renewable energy sources (default: false)
- `validFrom` — (required) offer validity start date, ISO 8601 format
- `validUntil` — (optional) offer validity end date, ISO 8601 format
- `termsUrl` — (optional) URL to the full terms and conditions document
- `target` — (optional) Enum: `personal`, `business`, `both`. Target audience for this offer (default: `both`)
- `highlights` — (optional) array of strings, key selling points displayed on the offer card
- `supplierId` — (required) UUID of the supplier providing this offer
- `offerCode` — (optional) unique internal offer code, max 50 characters
- `offerStatus` — (optional) Enum: `draft`, `active`, `expiring`, `expired`, `archived`. Defaults to `draft`

Offer is created with `draft` status. Use the Change Status endpoint to publish it.

Requires **admin** role.
